neighborhoods.name: Uptown Saint John
neighborhoods.geoTrait: brick-and-stone heritage core on the harbour hill
neighborhoods.hook: Saint John's historic core — the brick-and-stone streetscape rebuilt after the Great Fire of 1877, wrapped around the City Market and King Street.
facts: Rebuilt in brick and stone after the Great Fire of 1877 destroyed much of the old wooden town
Saint John City Market is Canada's oldest continuing farmers' market
King Street and Prince William Street anchor the heritage retail core
Imperial Theatre and King's Square sit at its centre
One of Canada's best-preserved 19th-century commercial districts

neighborhoods.name: the North End
neighborhoods.displayName: the North End of Saint John
neighborhoods.geoTrait: older harbour-side district below the Saint John Throughway
neighborhoods.hook: Saint John's historic working district north of uptown — older harbour-side housing along Main Street, hard hit by the highway era and now slowly reinvesting.
facts: Historic working-class district north of the uptown peninsula
Older housing stock along Main Street, much of it pre-war
Divided from uptown when the Saint John Throughway was built through it
Overlooks the harbour and the Reversing Falls approaches

neighborhoods.name: the South End
neighborhoods.displayName: the South End of Saint John
neighborhoods.geoTrait: waterfront peninsula tip of dense Victorian rowhousing
neighborhoods.hook: The waterfront tip of the Saint John peninsula — dense Victorian rowhousing between uptown and the harbour, one of the city's most historic residential quarters.
facts: Occupies the southern tip of the uptown peninsula on the harbour
Dense Victorian and Edwardian rowhousing, much rebuilt after the 1877 fire
Fronts the working harbour and the port
Home to Rockwood-era heritage streets and the waterfront

neighborhoods.name: the West Side
neighborhoods.displayName: the West Side of Saint John
neighborhoods.geoTrait: harbour's west shore around the Reversing Falls
neighborhoods.hook: Saint John's West Side — the Lancaster and Carleton communities across the harbour, home to the Reversing Falls Rapids and the Carleton Martello Tower.
facts: Comprises the historic Carleton and Lancaster communities west of the harbour
The Reversing Falls Rapids sit where the Saint John River meets the Bay of Fundy here
Carleton Martello Tower National Historic Site overlooks the harbour
Anchored around Simms Corner and the west-side waterfront

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Fredericton
neighborhoods.geoTrait: riverfront Garrison District on the Saint John River
neighborhoods.hook: Fredericton's riverfront heart — the historic Garrison District, Officers' Square and Queen Street, where the capital's government and heritage core meet the Saint John River.
facts: Built around the historic Garrison District and Officers' Square
Queen Street is the capital's main heritage commercial spine
Beaverbrook Art Gallery and the provincial legislature sit here
Fronts the Saint John River, with flood-aware riverfront blocks after 2018 and 2019
Christ Church Cathedral anchors the east downtown

neighborhoods.name: Marysville
neighborhoods.geoTrait: planned 19th-century cotton-mill town on the Nashwaak River
neighborhoods.hook: Fredericton's north-side company town — a planned 19th-century cotton-mill village on the Nashwaak River, and one of Canada's best-preserved industrial National Historic Sites.
facts: Marysville is a National Historic Site of Canada
Built as a planned cotton-mill company town by Alexander 'Boss' Gibson in the 1800s
Rows of brick mill-worker housing and the great brick cotton mill survive
Sits on the Nashwaak River on Fredericton's north side
Amalgamated into Fredericton but keeps a distinct village identity

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Moncton
neighborhoods.geoTrait: Main Street core on the bend of the tidal Petitcodiac
neighborhoods.hook: Moncton's Main Street core on the bend of the tidal Petitcodiac — the Avenir Centre, Resurgo Place and the downtown that anchors Atlantic Canada's fastest-growing city.
facts: Centred on Main Street along the tidal Petitcodiac River
The Avenir Centre arena anchors the downtown entertainment district
Resurgo Place museum tells the city's railway story
The Petitcodiac tidal bore runs past the downtown riverfront
Bilingual commercial core of the Greater Moncton region
